config.plugin.name: pgoutput
config.slot.name: ${SOURCE_PG_SLOT_NAME}
config.publication.name: ${SOURCE_PG_PUBLICATION}
config._comment.converters: JSON converters chosen for ClickHouse compatibility. JSON is natively supported by ClickHouse Kafka engine, human-readable in Kafka UI, and avoids the complexity of Avro schema management.
config._comment.decimal_handling: double is lossy for financial NUMERIC columns but ClickHouse has no native NUMERIC type. Use 'precise' (string) if exact decimal fidelity is required downstream.
config._comment.snapshot_mode: when_needed behaves like 'initial' when offsets are valid, but also runs a fresh snapshot when stored offsets point to an LSN no longer in WAL (e.g., after slot invalidation or stale connect-offsets that survived a connector delete). This makes recovery paths robust without requiring the script to fight Kafka Connect's offset persistence.
config._comment.signal: Source signal channel: Debezium reads rows from public.debezium_signal to trigger ad-hoc actions like incremental snapshots (see scripts/connect/snapshot-tables.sh). The signal table must be in the publication AND in table.include.list — register-connector.sh appends it automatically so users only configure SOURCE_PG_TABLE_ALLOWLIST. The value for signal.data.collection uses Debezium's schema.table form for the PostgreSQL connector (no database prefix).
config.heartbeat.action.query: INSERT INTO public.reporting_heartbeat (ts) VALUES (NOW()) ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET ts = NOW()
